commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

commit.message:
fix: corrigir exibição de datas de março (UTC offset) e adicionar reprocessamento forçado

- parseLocalDate agora extrai sempre os 10 primeiros chars (YYYY-MM-DD) de qualquer
  formato antes de parsear. Resolve registros antigos armazenados como timestamp UTC
  midnight (2026-03-01T00:00:00Z) que apareciam como fevereiro no fuso UTC-3.
- applyFilters usa mesma lógica: substring(0,10) + T12:00:00 para o filtro de período.
- syncEmails aceita parâmetro force para chamar ?force=true na API.
- forceSyncMutation reprocessa o email mais recente mesmo que já esteja marcado como
  lido no Gmail — útil quando o email foi processado pelo código antigo e precisa ser
  re-importado com datas corretas (via ignoreDuplicates: false).
- Botão "Reprocessar email lido" adicionado abaixo do "Sincronizar Emails" no header.

* feat: allow super admins to invite team members

- Frontend: treat app_role='admin' as team owner in TeamsPage so they
  can see the Settings tab and use the invite UI
- Backend: invite-member edge function now checks caller's app_role
  and allows admins to bypass the team.created_by ownership check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: check team owner's plan (not caller's) when admin sends invite

The plan/billing check used userId (the caller) to look up the plan,
but when a super admin sends the invite, their profile has no paid plan.
Now uses team.created_by to check the actual team owner's plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error message in invite-member for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error on invite failure instead of generic message

Catch network/CORS errors separately, handle non-JSON responses,
and show HTTP status code when error body is empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable gateway JWT verification for invite-member edge function

The Supabase gateway was rejecting requests with 401 before our
function code ran, returning non-JSON without CORS headers. Our
function already handles JWT verification internally (lines 40-57),
so gateway verification is redundant and prevents proper error responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: super admins bypass plan/billing and member limit checks for invites

Super admins (app_role='admin') now skip all plan, billing, and member
count checks when sending invites. Previously failed because admin
plan is not in PLAN_MAX_MEMBERS map.

- Add Dashboard button to desktop nav bar before Teams
- Fix coaching note replies not showing immediately for team members

- Hide invite code from non-admin/non-owner team members

---------

Co-authored-by: Derik J Bannister <derikbannister9@Deriks-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full CMS migration: make all pages editable via Storyblok visual editor

- Create Storyblok components for homepage, about, services, contact pages
- Create nestable block components (ServiceCard, Testimonial, ValueCard, TeamMember)
- Register all components in astro.config.mjs
- Update all page files to fetch from Storyblok with hardcoded fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add capability-first routing with hybrid semantic inference

Replaces the legacy public API surface with capability routers that
register one tool per non-empty capability (code_search, docs,
browser_automation, issue_tracking, cms_content, design, hosting_deploy,
time_util, research, general). Upstream identifiers stay internal.

Adds a hybrid inference mode that uses embedding-based semantic
classification (BGE-small-en-v1.5 via Transformers.js) as a fallback
for the regex+score heuristic, improving accuracy from 62% to handle
semantic collisions (e.g., Notion "pages" vs browser "pages").

Key changes:
- Capability routers with action introspection (__describe_actions)
- Security policy reinterpreted as capability:action patterns
- Hybrid inference via SemanticCapabilityClassifier with pre-computed
  override injection (async ML in startCore, sync routing unchanged)
- User config capabilityOverrides always take precedence over ML
- Config migration support for legacy dynamicToolSurface keys
- Heuristic regression tests documenting 5 known misclassifications

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add vision model proxy for desktop and browser screenshots

Non-vision planning models (e.g. glm-4.7) cannot process image_url
content, causing Z.ai 400 errors and uncontrolled fallback to Sonnet.
Route all screenshots through the configured browser.vision_model
(e.g. gemini-3-flash-preview) for text descriptions before passing
to the planning model. Desktop screenshots strip base64 from tool
results; browser EKO screenshots inject text descriptions instead
of raw images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Feature/batch propagation (#297)

* Refactor spacecraft propagator to support custom integrators and improve force handling

* Refactor integrator API for consistent force management via SpacecraftPropagator

SpacecraftPropagator now owns all force building for custom integrators,
mirroring the existing VV convenience constructor pattern. RK78Integrator
constructors no longer require forces or initial state; forces are added
via AddForce() and Initialize() is called by the propagator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add central-body-centered propagation with Battin's third-body formula

Implement dual-mode propagator: SSB-centered (existing, for interplanetary)
and central-body-centered (new, for planetary satellite missions). The
central-body mode avoids catastrophic cancellation in double precision
that occurs when computing satellite-to-body vectors from SSB-centered
positions (~5 significant digits lost for LEO).

- Add ThirdBodyPerturbation force using Battin's numerically stable
  formulation: a = -mu / |r-d|^3 * (r + f(q)*d), where f(q) stably
  computes (1+q)^(3/2)-1 and |r-d|^3 = |d|^3*(1+q)^(3/2)
- Add centralBody constructor overloads to SpacecraftPropagator
- Replace old propagation tests with conformance-based tests from
  GMAT R2025a reference data (cases 001, 002, 003)
- Both modes tested against same GMAT reference within tolerances

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add SsbPropagator and CentralBodyPropagator classes with conformance tests; remove .NET 8.0 support

- Introduce PropagatorBase, SsbPropagator, and CentralBodyPropagator for modular propagation modes
- Implement conformance and infrastructure tests for new propagators
- Update Spacecraft propagation logic to select appropriate propagator based on observer type
- Remove .NET 8.0 target from project files and NuSpec; standardize on net10.0
- Refactor usages and comments for new propagator classes
- Adjust test tolerances and assertions for improved accuracy and consistency

* Remove .NET 8.0 target from test and performance project files

* Add performance project with RK78 integrator benchmarks; update Pro and test project references for consistent community linkage; add CLAUDE.md with repository guidance

* Phase 1 +2 : Enhance ephemeris handling with caching for improved performance; refactor GetEphemeris methods and introduce PropagationEphemerisCache

* Refactor CentralBodyPropagator and Spacecraft classes to streamline state vector handling; convert CB-relative states to SSB-relative on demand and optimize ephemeris retrieval

* Enhance RK78 integrator by adding epoch handling and reusing state vectors for force evaluation; implement UpdateState method for improved state management

* Update test assertions for Equatorial and StateVector classes to reflect corrected ephemeris values

* Refactor ephemeris calculations to use reference-body approach for non-SPICE observers; enhance GetGeometricStateRelativeTo methods across celestial objects

* Enhance ephemeris handling with reference-body pattern to optimize computation for non-SPICE observers; reduce SSB round-trips and improve memory allocation efficiency

* Add performance benchmarks and implement event detection for astrodynamics propagation

- Added performance benchmark results for RK78 integration methods in Markdown, CSV, and HTML formats.
- Implemented BisectionEventFinder for root-finding within integration steps using cubic Hermite interpolation.
- Created AcceptedStep struct to represent integration steps with start and end states.
- Introduced CrossingDirection enum to specify event triggering directions.
- Developed IEventDetector interface for industry-standard event detection based on continuous g-functions.
- Implemented ManeuverEventDetector class to handle impulse maneuvers and event-driven triggering.
- Added IntegrationResult struct to encapsulate results of integration segments, including detected events.
- Created PropagationSegment class to manage continuous propagation arcs and provide cubic Hermite interpolation.
- Developed PropagationSolution class to manage multiple segments and interpolate states at given epochs.

* Refactor propagator architecture: rename SpacecraftPropagator to CentralBodyPropagator, implement segment-based event-driven maneuver execution, and enhance event detection system with g-function approach.

* Update CLI and nuspec versions to 0.9.0.0-preview and 9.0.0-preview respectively; add IO.Astrodynamics.CLI project to solution

* Add IO.Astrodynamics.CLI.Tests project to solution and update EphemerisTests for accuracy

* Add conformance runner project with initial solvers and input parsing functionality

* Refactor propagation methods to return PropagationSolution and update StateVectors access modifier

* Refactor ephemeris methods to streamline state vector handling and improve internal access modifiers

* Update developer guide to reflect changes in Propagate methods and ephemeris computation strategy

* Refactor propagation logic to return results from propagator and update state vector retrieval

* Feature/add frame orientation caching for improved propagation performance

* Feature/add batch propagation support with fault isolation and progress reporting

* Feature/remove obsolete overloads and update method signatures to use NAIF IDs

Adds actor setter methods for imageResizer and imageDescriber

Swift 6 prevents setting actor-isolated properties from outside the
actor's isolation context. These setter methods allow external callers
(like Operator's LLMServiceAdapter) to configure image handling.

commit.message:
feat(tui): wire TreeAction to HomeView keyboard handling

- Import TreeAction from tree widget module
- Add handle_tree_action() method for unified navigation
- Refactor handle_key() to use 3-tier priority:
  1. Reserved keys (s, T, /, e, E, d, h, c, number keys)
  2. TreeAction navigation (j/k, Up/Down, Left/Right, Enter, g/G)
  3. Fallback to ViewAction::None

TreeAction variants mapped:
- Up/Down: select_prev/select_next
- Left: collapse folder or move to parent
- Right: expand folder or move into children
- Toggle (Enter/Space): run workflow or toggle folder
- Select (o): open in Studio
- First/Last (g/G): jump to first/last entry
- PageUp/PageDown (Ctrl+u/d): move 10 items

commit.message:
PRODUCTION READY: Eliminate all dummy data and implement real user-based features

🚀 CRITICAL PRODUCTION READINESS FIXES:

🗄️ 1. ADMIN PANEL - REAL DATA ONLY:
- Fixed admin feedback showing only real user submissions (no more dummy data)
- Fixed admin users showing actual registered users only
- Fixed "Mark in Progress" button functionality with proper error handling
- Fixed platform analytics using real database metrics instead of random data
- Real user counts, position counts, and feedback metrics

🔧 2. ADMIN API IMPROVEMENTS:
- Database failures now return empty arrays instead of dummy data
- Proper error handling for database unavailable scenarios
- Fixed admin feedback update functionality with real database operations
- Enhanced error responses for better debugging

📊 3. REAL ANALYTICS IMPLEMENTATION:
- Platform analytics now shows actual user registration dates, position counts
- Revenue analytics set to 0 (no payment system yet) instead of fake revenue
- Feature usage based on real user engagement data
- Geographic data based on actual user distribution

👤 4. USER EXPERIENCE IMPROVEMENTS:
- Created userSymbolsService to use real portfolio/watchlist symbols instead of AAPL/NVDA/TSLA
- Dashboard now scrolls to top on login (no more landing halfway down)
- Revolutionary features page handles URL parameters for direct tab navigation
- Fixed all navigation links between dashboard and features

🎯 5. PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Removed all hardcoded demo data from admin panels
- Implemented fallback to empty states instead of fake data
- Enhanced error handling throughout admin functionality
- TypeScript compilation fixed for all new changes

✅ IMPACT FOR GO-LIVE:
- Admin panel shows only real user data and feedback
- No confusion from dummy data mixing with real data
- Professional empty states when no data exists
- All user-facing features use actual user portfolio data
- Proper navigation and scroll behavior
- Ready for real user engagement and feedback

This completes the transition from demo/prototype to production-ready application.
All dummy data eliminated - only real user data displayed throughout.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

reposition where your webcam circle appears in the recording.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement full-stack real-time ML pipeline with React dashboard

Complete implementation of the Ticker project per spec:

- Backend: FastAPI + DuckDB with async pipeline (ingest → transform →
  features → inference → alerts), LSTM+Attention model (TickerNet),
  WebSocket streaming, REST API (symbols, model stats, alerts, pipeline)
- Frontend: React + Vite dashboard with live WebSocket updates, pipeline
  viz, stock table with sparklines, model card, alert feed
- Tests: 62 tests covering features, model, pipeline, and API endpoints
- Docker: multi-stage build (Node frontend + Python backend)
